thread_raster_war_intro.topic: Raster timing on NTSC vs PAL — who's right?

messages.text: Anyone else getting +2 cycle drift on PAL C64s? My raster splits are off by exactly 2 cycles on every PAL board I've tested.
PAL = 312 lines, NTSC = 263. If you're targeting the same raster line number, the cycles per line are different. 63 vs 64.
Right, so a hard-coded raster delay macro is brittle. Should we ship a detection stub?

choices.text: Join the debate with your own measurements

thread_raster_war_intro.originalTopic: Raster timing on NTSC vs PAL — who's right?

messages.text: Pssst. Future Crew is looking for a tracker to score their next mega-demo. Someone from Tricycle Crews would be a perfect fit.
If you can get an audience, this is a once-in-a-lifetime collab. They release gold at Assembly every year.

thread_sid_filter_leak.topic: SID filter sweep — undocumented register trick

messages.text: Did anyone else know SID register $D418 has a resonance sweep that no one documents? I've been abusing it for slides all week.
Wait, that register is the filter cutoff. Not sweep. You can mod the resonance? Are you sure it's not your hardware?
100% reproducible. I'll post a tiny test program tonight.

choices.text: Reproduce the trick and report back

thread_sid_filter_leak.originalTopic: SID filter sweep — undocumented register trick

thread_amiga_blitter_bench.topic: Amiga Blitter vs CPU loop — who wins on AGA?

messages.text: Just benchmarked a 320x256 chunky-to-planar on A1200. Blitter queue hits 14.5MB/s, my best 68k loop hits 9.2. Anyone beat that?
For a 4-plane blit your numbers are correct. The blitter is faster on the 4-channel case but loses on 1-channel (no bus contention). What about interleaved?

choices.text: Propose a collaboration on a blitter bench utility

thread_amiga_blitter_bench.originalTopic: Amiga Blitter vs CPU loop — who wins on AGA?
